  "findings": [
    "Brazing performed without nitrogen purge",
    "Black cupric oxide scale present inside refrigerant piping"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately",
    "Cut out and replace the contaminated copper piping sections",
    "Re-braze new joints while maintaining a continuous low-pressure nitrogen purge"
  ],
  "rationale": "Brazing copper refrigerant lines without a nitrogen purge allows oxygen to react with the heated copper, forming black cupric oxide scale. This scale will flake off, contaminate the system, clog filter-driers, and damage the compressor. The contaminated piping must be cut out and replaced before evacuation or charging can proceed.",
  "references": [
    "EPA Section 608",
    "ACCA Standard 5 (HVAC Quality Installation Specification)",
    "Manufacturer Installation Specifications"
  ]
}
